title: Saudi Arabian Duck Legs Confit
description: A rich, creamy, and peppery brunch dish featuring slow-cooked duck legs, perfect for a special occasion. Inspired by traditional Saudi Arabian flavors.
instructions: Rinse the duck legs and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, and salt.
Rub the spice mixture all over the duck legs, making sure to coat them evenly.
Heat coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sear the duck legs until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the duck legs from the pot and set them aside.
In the same pot, add minced garlic and grated ginger. Cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the yogurt, lemon juice, and paprika to the pot. Stir to combine.
Return the duck legs to the pot and make sure they're mostly covered with the yogurt sauce.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2-3 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls off the bone.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve warm.
